channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AppliedXL and Associated Press team up to use AI to sift through federal regulations
item.description: In an era when local newsrooms are stretched thinner than ever, keeping up with the constant deluge of government regulations and updates can feel impossible.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Drone delivery service Wing teams with Serve to expand delivery in Dallas
item.description: Serve Robotics, which makes autonomous sidewalk delivery bots, and Wing, Alphabet’s on-demand drone delivery service, announced a pilot partnership on Tuesday meant to expand autonomous last-mile food delivery in Dallas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: PayPal wants to share your data – unless you do this
item.description: The change of policy has been enabled by default, meaning you need to opt out if you care about your privacy. Here's how.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Sony launches funky new LinkBuds earbuds with customizable cases – and a new Bluetooth speaker to match
item.description: Sony's beautifully odd LinkBuds get an audio upgrade, more personalization, and a clever little speaker too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Does your Halloween rager really need a Partiful invite?
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Scammers’ sneaky new tactic preying on people who’ve lost their iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to protect yourself with eight steps if your iPhone is lost or stolen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US modifies AI chip export rules, boosting Middle East access
item.description: In a move aimed at facilitating the global development and supply of artificial intelligence (AI), the US Commerce Department has brought a new policy that will ease export restrictions for advanced AI chips to data centers in the Middle East and Central Asia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rushing for AI ROI? Chances are it will cost you
item.description: Many organizations have struggled to find the ROI after launching AI projects, but there’s a danger in demanding too much too soon, according to IT research and advisory firm Forrester.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Verizon customers report major outage on mobile network
item.description: Tens of thousands of Verizon users reported outages for the wireless carrier’s services across the U.S. on Monday, with Chicago and Indianapolis among the hardest-hit cities and some iPhone users stuck in “SOS” mode. According to tracking website Downdetector.com, the outage began at around 9:30 a.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: Meteorologists calculate that more than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok owner ByteDance may use a domestic source to develop a new AI model
item.description: TikTok’s Chinese parent ByteDance plans to develop an AI model trained primarily with chips from compatriot Huawei Technologies, said three people familiar with the matter, as U.S. curbs turn the social media giant homeward in search of chips.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Waymo self-driving car interrupts Vice President Harris’ motorcade in San Francisco
item.description: Harris, the Democratic presidential nominee, was in the city staying overnight Friday at the Fairmont Hotel before a campaign fundraiser at the San Francisco Palace of Fine Arts Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kristin J. Bender, The Mercury News

item.title: DirecTV, Dish to merge to create largest US pay-TV provider
item.description: Under the terms of the transaction, DirecTV will acquire Dish TV and Sling TV from EchoStar Corp. for a nominal consideration of $1 plus the assumption of about $9.75 billion of debt.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Salesforce: Latest news and insights
item.description: Salesforce is a vendor of cloud-based software and applications for sales, customer service, marketing automation, ecommerce, analytics, and application development. Based in San Francisco, Calif.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The CHIPS Act money: A timeline of grants to chipmakers
item.description: The CHIPS and Science Act provides $52.7 billion for American semiconductor research, development, manufacturing, and workforce development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Android's latest nightmare: millions of devices infected by sneaky malware
item.description: There have been cases when widely downloaded apps from the Play Store are infected with malware. Kurt the CyberGuy tackles the issue of a new Trojan malware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lionsgate's bold move into AI is about to change filmmaking forever
item.description: Lionsgate's revolutionary move to partner with tech company Runway to integrate AI into its production process is going to take the film industry by storm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Examining the many ways to run loops on Linux
item.description: The bash shell provides numerous ways to loop through data to make your work – especially scripting challenges – easier. You can loop through a pile of data so you can get a lot done just by running a single script, and you can generate and loop through different sequences of values.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Zero Trust + AI: A match made in the clouds
item.description: Have any terms in the cybersecurity space generated more buzz than “zero trust” and “AI”? At the risk of sounding like a Magic 8 Ball, my reply is no.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Demystifying AI for the public sector
item.description: Government agencies are understandably hesitant about artificial intelligence. But with the right partners, AI can revolutionize government services. The post Demystifying AI for the public sector first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Journalist booted from X after publishing hacked Vance document
item.description: Klippenstein published the 271-page report compiled by the Trump campaign to vet Vance on his Substack.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.), chairwoman of the Oversight and Accountability Subcommittee on Cybersecurity, IT and Innovation, is asking for a briefing by Oct. 3. The post Rep. Mace questions GSA’s plan for replacing FedRAMP JAB first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: There’s more in the defense industrial base than the Defense Department may be aware of
item.description: "One of the problems is the Defense Department doesn't use the software and data that all first-class enterprises in the world use," Jeb Nadaner said. The post There’s more in the defense industrial base than the Defense Department may be aware of first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Using Instagram's new teen account settings to supervise young users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Worried about your teen's Instagram use? Faced with growing pressure to do more to stop online harm, the social media platform has rolled out a major new update to protect young people and give parents more control.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: X releases its first transparency report since Elon Musk's takeover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Social media platform X on Wednesday published its first transparency report since the company was purchased by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top 4 reasons you might still need VPN with cellular service
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains why you may still need a virtual private network even if you're using your phone's cellular network.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Can AI make video games more immersive? Some studios turn to AI-fueled NPCs for more interaction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — For decades, video games have relied on scripted, stilted interactions with non-player characters to help shepherd gamers in their journeys.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NASA develops an in-house team for the tricky information technology projects
item.description: "We want to accelerate missions, and we do so by bringing human centered design and advanced modern digital techniques to the agency," Shawn Chittle said. The post NASA develops an in-house team for the tricky information technology projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok removes Russian state media accounts for 'covert influence operations' ahead of US election
item.description: TikTok has removed accounts associated with Russian state media for engaging in “covert influence operations” ahead of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5
